entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: Glycine max L. cv. Akishirome, the crop studied for carbon and nitrogen translocation.

entities.name: CARBON TRANSLOCATION
entities.description: The movement of carbon (13C) from source to sink within the plant.

entities.name: NITROGEN TRANSLOCATION
entities.description: The movement of nitrogen (15N) from source to sink within the plant.

entities.name: 13CO2 FEEDING
entities.description: The process of feeding the plant with 13C-labeled carbon dioxide to track carbon movement.

entities.name: 15NO3 FEEDING
entities.description: The process of feeding the plant with 15N-labeled nitrate to track nitrogen movement.

entities.name: NITROGEN APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of nitrogen at low (2 g N m-2) and high (35 g N m-2) levels in the basal medium.

entities.name: DEPODDING
entities.description: The removal of all pods from the plant except the pod of the source-sink unit.

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total weight of the plant organs.

entities.name: SINK DEMAND
entities.description: The requirement for nutrients by sink organs which influences partitioning.

entities.name: SOURCE-SINK UNIT
entities.description: A unit consisting of a trifoliated leaf, the axillary pod, and the subtending internode.

relationships.description: is the subject of study for
is the subject of study for
nullifies partitioning outside the unit at low-N
